theory.intro: Unknown angles at one point are found by matching the diagram to a known angle total or equality and stating the reason.
facts: Angles on a **straight line** add to $180^{\circ}$.
Angles in a **right angle** add to $90^{\circ}$.

foundation.question_text:
[tikz]
\begin{tikzpicture}[scale=0.7]\draw (-2,0)--(2,0);\draw (0,0)--(68:1.8);\node at (-0.75,0.38) {$x$};\node at (0.65,0.35) {$68^{\circ}$};\end{tikzpicture}
[/tikz]
Find $x$ and give a reason.

[tikz]
\begin{tikzpicture}[scale=0.7]\draw (0,0)--(2,0);\draw (0,0)--(0,2);\draw (0,0)--(37:1.8);\node at (0.75,0.25) {$37^{\circ}$};\node at (0.3,0.9) {$x$};\end{tikzpicture}
[/tikz]
Find $x$ and give a reason.

[tikz]
\begin{tikzpicture}[scale=0.7]\draw (-2,-0.7)--(2,0.7);\draw (-2,0.7)--(2,-0.7);\node at (0,0.55) {$145^{\circ}$};\node at (0,-0.55) {$x$};\end{tikzpicture}
[/tikz]
Find $x$ and give a reason.

[tikz]
\begin{tikzpicture}[scale=0.7]\draw (0,0)--(2,0);\draw (0,0)--(145:2);\draw (145:0.65) arc[start angle=145,end angle=360,radius=0.65];\node at (0.25,0.75) {$145^{\circ}$};\node at (0,-0.85) {$x$};\end{tikzpicture}
[/tikz]
Find $x$ and give a reason.

development.question_text:
[tikz]
\begin{tikzpicture}[scale=0.7]\draw (-2,0)--(2,0);\draw (0,0)--(75:1.8);\draw (0,0)--(138:1.8);\node at (-0.88,0.34) {$42^{\circ}$};\node at (-0.27,0.9) {$63^{\circ}$};\node at (0.72,0.55) {$x$};\end{tikzpicture}
[/tikz]
Find $x$ and give a reason.

[tikz]
\begin{tikzpicture}[scale=0.7]\draw (0,0)--(2,0);\draw (0,0)--(0,2);\draw (0,0)--(28:1.8);\draw (0,0)--(61:1.8);\node at (0.95,0.22) {$28^{\circ}$};\node at (0.78,0.72) {$33^{\circ}$};\node at (0.18,1.12) {$x$};\end{tikzpicture}
[/tikz]
Find $x$ and give a reason.

[tikz]
\begin{tikzpicture}[scale=0.7]\foreach \a in {0,80,205}{\draw (0,0)--(\a:2);}\node at (0.75,0.45) {$80^{\circ}$};\node at (-0.4,0.7) {$148^{\circ}$};\node at (0,-0.75) {$x$};\end{tikzpicture}
[/tikz]
Find $x$ and give a reason.
Two lines intersect. One angle is $3x+8$ degrees and its vertically opposite angle is $86^{\circ}$. Find $x$.
On a straight line, two adjacent angles are equal. Find each angle.

mastery.question_text:
[tikz]
\begin{tikzpicture}[scale=0.7]\draw (-2,-1)--(2,1);\draw (-2,1)--(2,-1);\draw (0,0)--(90:1.8);\node at (-0.3,0.65) {$38^{\circ}$};\node at (0.45,0.55) {$47^{\circ}$};\node at (0,-0.65) {$x$};\end{tikzpicture}
[/tikz]
Two lines intersect. A third ray splits one angle into $38^{\circ}$ and $47^{\circ}$. Find the angle vertically opposite the whole split angle.
Four angles meet at a point. Two vertically opposite angles are each $124^{\circ}$; the other two are equal. Find either remaining angle.
